eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Europe at 71.5% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, driven by UEFA's 16 qualifiers including top betting favorites Spain, France, and England, bolstered by recent March playoff triumphs for Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia, Sweden, and Türkiye that added depth without major dilution. South America's 20.5% reflects CONMEBOL powerhouses Argentina and Brazil securing spots earlier, though their six-team contingent faces stiffer paths amid Europe's volume. Lower odds for Africa (3.5%), Asia (3.0%), North America (2.5%), and Oceania (0.3%) stem from historical knockout limitations despite expanded slots, with final intercontinental playoff wins like Congo DR and Iraq providing minimal uplift as groups and schedules finalize.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) hit (HIGH) $320 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Apple (AAPL) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Apple (AAPL) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Apple (AAPL) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Apple's stock has rallied to all-time highs above $294 last week, propelled by its fiscal Q2 2026 earnings beat on April 30—$111.2 billion revenue up 17% year-over-year and $2.01 diluted EPS surpassing estimates—fueled by iPhone Pro demand and Services growth amid AI optimism. Trading around $293 as of May 11, AAPL benefits from reports of an Apple-Intel chip production deal boosting chipmaker sentiment. Analyst consensus price target of $305 reflects expectations for continued margin expansion. Traders eye WWDC 2026 (June 8 keynote) for iOS 27 and Siri enhancements as pivotal catalysts, alongside Q3 earnings in late July, with macroeconomic factors like Treasury yields influencing valuation multiples.

markets.question: Will "Michael" be the April film with the highest domestic gross on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the title of the film released in April 2026 with the highest domestic gross between opening and May 30, 2026 according to "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page.

In the event of an exact tie the film that comes first alphabetically will be considered the winner.

If there is no final data available by June 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

events.title: Highest Domestically Grossing April Film on May 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the title of the film released in April 2026 with the highest domestic gross between opening and May 30, 2026 according to "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page.

In the event of an exact tie the film that comes first alphabetically will be considered the winner.

If there is no final data available by June 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie commands a 99.1% implied probability as the highest domestically grossing April 2026 release on May 31, backed by its towering $412 million domestic haul through early May—over $208 million ahead of runner-up Michael ($204 million)—fueled by a record-shattering $132 million opening weekend, enduring family appeal from Illumination's Nintendo animation prowess, and three straight box office #1s. Recent legs remain strong, adding $6.6 million last weekend amid new competition like Mortal Kombat II, with global grosses surpassing $940 million on May 10 signaling sustained momentum. Michael’s biopic buzz delivered a potent $97 million debut but front-loaded drops limit catch-up potential; realistic upsets would require Super Mario controversy or Michael multipliers exceeding 3x its opening—highly improbable given historical animated blockbuster precedents. Traders eye Memorial Day boosts before May 31 resolution.

markets.question: Will Greece win the first Eurovision Semi-Final?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" for the listed country whose candidate wins the most points at the Eurovision First Semi-Final, scheduled for May 12, 2026.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win the most points at the Eurovision 2026 First Semi-Final based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If this competition is not held or no ranking is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of the First Semi-Final for Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

markets.question: Citrea FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Citrea's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

Only an official token launched by Citrea will qualify. Stablecoins, memecoins, LSTs and synthetic tokens will not count.

The token must be actively and publicly tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Citrea (https://x.com/citrea_xyz) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic Party veteran Choo Mi-ae commands 95% trader consensus for the June 3, 2026 Gyeonggi Province gubernatorial election following her decisive April 7 primary victory over incumbent Gov. Kim Dong-yeon and Han Jun-ho, unifying the ruling party's base in this Democratic stronghold. A May 6 poll confirmed her lead over People Power Party nominee Yang Hyang-ja, beyond the margin of error, amid her active campaigning on semiconductors, housing, and balanced development. Residual odds on Yoo Seung-min reflect earlier recruitment speculation he rebuffed. With three weeks remaining, late scandals, gaffes, or opposition surges could challenge her, though Gyeonggi's historical DP dominance poses high barriers.

markets.question: Will Google have the #2 AI model at the end of June 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Texas's 38th Congressional District, encompassing Republican-leaning Houston suburbs like Cypress, Tomball, and Greater Katy with a Cook PVI of R+10, drives trader consensus favoring the Republican Party at 83.5% implied probability, consistent with Solid Republican ratings from Cook Political Report and historical GOP victories by 25+ points in 2022 and 2024. Incumbent Wesley Hunt vacated the seat for a U.S. Senate bid, but the March 3 primaries solidified Democrat Melissa McDonough as nominee after her 51.6% win, while Republicans head to a May 26 runoff between Trump-endorsed Jon Bonck (47.7% in primary) and Shelly deZevallos. GOP fundraising dominance—over $2 million combined versus McDonough's $48,000—bolsters the edge ahead of the November general election.

markets.question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

eventMetadata.context_description: Somaliland's House of Elders (Guurti) approved a 27-month extension of House of Representatives and local council terms on April 28, 2026, citing security concerns, drought, and National Electoral Commission (NEC) technical challenges, postponing parliamentary elections originally slated for May 2026 into late 2028. This recent decision drives trader consensus at 78.5% for no election before 2027, amid criticism from President Abdirahman Mohamed Abdullahi (Irro) of Waddani Party—fresh off its 2024 presidential victory—over risks to democratic credibility. Waddani leads conditional odds at 29.4% on presidential momentum, while former ruling Kulmiye trails at 1.6% post-defeat, and UCID at 5.2%, reflecting limited prospects absent a snap vote reversal.

markets.question: Will Petr Yan be the next fighter to be ranked first in the UFC Pound-For-Pound Rankings in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings, following Islam Makhachev.

If Islam Makhachev remains #1 in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings through December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the Pound-For-Pound rankings within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Islam/No Next #1 in 2026”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Ilia Topuria at 59% implied probability to become UFC pound-for-pound #1 in 2026, driven by his undefeated 17-0 record as lightweight champion, including recent knockouts over Max Holloway and Charles Oliveira that solidified his #2 P4P ranking post-UFC 317. Islam Makhachev holds firm at #1 as welterweight champion with an unmatched resume of title defenses, supporting the 39.5% on "Islam/None in 2026" amid his planned August return. Alexander Volkanovski's 31.9% reflects his January unanimous decision title retention over Diego Lopes at featherweight, restoring top-5 P4P status. Arman Tsarukyan (29.9%) gains from his November arm-triangle submission of Dan Hooker, cementing lightweight contender momentum, while Alex Pereira's 22.9% stems from light heavyweight dominance despite post-UFC 328 ranking stability.

markets.question: 1H Spread: Cavaliers (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the first half of the NBA game between Pistons and Cavaliers, scheduled for May 11 at 8:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Cavaliers" if the Cavaliers are winning by 2 or more points at halftime.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Pistons".

The result will be determined based on the score at halftime only.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Pistons vs. Cavaliers
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 11 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Pistons win, the market will resolve to "Pistons".
If the Cavaliers win, the market will resolve to "Cavaliers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the Eastern Conference Semifinals series at 2-1 in favor of the top-seeded Detroit Pistons, Game 4 at Cleveland carries heightened stakes as the Cavaliers seek to even the matchup on home court following their 116-109 Game 3 victory, fueled by Donovan Mitchell's 35 points and James Harden's clutch scoring. Detroit's elite defense—allowing just 99.8 points per game—stifled Cleveland in the first two road wins (111-101, 107-97), powered by Cade Cunningham's playmaking, but faces backcourt uncertainty with Kevin Huerter (adductor) and Caris LeVert (heel) listed questionable on the injury report. The fully healthy Cavaliers hold rest parity and crowd support, while Pistons aim to regain momentum before potential Game 5 return home.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Anthropic have the best Math AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Math" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Math" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/math-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent President Iliana Iotova leads trader consensus at 58% implied probability for Bulgaria's autumn 2026 presidential election, buoyed by her Bulgarian Socialist Party (BSP) nomination and alignment with former President Rumen Radev's Progressive Bulgaria, which secured a parliamentary majority in the April 19 snap election. Radev's landslide victory—triggered after his January resignation elevated Iotova—and her May 7 mandate to him for government formation have consolidated their bloc's dominance, enhancing her incumbency advantage in the two-round direct vote. Reformist We Continue the Change–Democratic Bulgaria (PP-DB) figures like ex-PM Nikolai Denkov (11.5%) and Sofia Mayor Vassil Terziev (10.3%) lag amid their coalition's weak parliamentary showing, with GERB's Rosen Zhelyazkov distant; candidate primaries and nominations loom as key catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the constructor team that achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping visit US before 2027?
markets.description:
If Xi Jinping visits the United States between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Xi Jinping physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the United States. Whether or not Xi Jinping enters US airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from the US or China, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump announce Todd Blanche as the next United States Attorney General by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual Donald Trump announces as his pick to be United States Attorney General. 

An announcement from Donald Trump or the Trump administration stating their intent to nominate a specific individual for United States Attorney General will suffice to resolve this market, regardless of whether a formal nomination actually occurs.

Qualifying announcements must explicitly present the relevant individual as the nominee or future nominee for Attorney General. Announcements of acting or interim appointments, or announcements which merely reveal potential candidates, will not qualify. Media reports, speculation, or other unofficial information will not qualify.

A formal presidential nomination of an individual to be United States Attorney General will also suffice to resolve this market. Formal nominations are defined as the submission of a nomination message to the U.S. Senate.

If Donald Trump does not announce his pick for the next United States Attorney General by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No announcement by June 30".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump and the Trump Administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Federal statute permits Blanche to serve up to 210 days without Senate confirmation—potentially past the market deadline—allowing delay amid competing priorities like ongoing DOJ investigations and geopolitical tensions. Lee Zeldin, current EPA administrator and early favorite per reports, leads candidates at 13% after dodging recent questions on the role, while Blanche at 10% bolsters his case through aggressive actions targeting Trump adversaries. Harmeet Dhillon and Texas AG Ken Paxton trail amid speculation, with Senate GOP holds like Sen. Thom Tillis' reservations adding confirmation risks.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Shanghai be 26°C on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will "Anthropic" be said during the next episode of the All-In Podcast?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during the next released episode of the All-In Podcast. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old episodes or prerecorded clips are aired where people are speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 72.5% implied probability for U.S. enactment of an AI safety bill before 2027, reflecting stalled federal legislative progress amid a pro-innovation Trump administration stance. The White House's March 2026 National AI Policy Framework urged Congress to prioritize light-touch sector-specific rules and preempt patchwork state laws, but no comprehensive bill has advanced in the 119th Congress, where dozens of narrower proposals—like the AI Guardrails Act and deepfake protections—remain stuck in committees. Recent state actions, including Iowa's chatbot safety law signed May 8, underscore federal inaction, with midterms looming in November potentially complicating lame-duck passage. Historical patterns show AI bills facing filibuster risks and partisan divides on regulation scope.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22 parliamentary elections, no party secured a majority in the 90-seat National Assembly, with Prime Minister Robert Golob's centre-left Freedom Movement (GS) taking 29 seats ahead of Janez Janša's right-wing Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) at 28, New Slovenia-Christian Democrats (NSi) at 9, and Democrats (D) at 6. Golob conceded failure to form a coalition on April 20 after a month of talks, prompting President Nataša Pirc Musar to shift consultations to Janša. On May 5, Janša announced broad agreement on coalition guidelines with potential partners including NSi, D, Fokus, and SLS, eyeing a minority government backed externally by Resnica. Parliament's vote on the prime minister-designate remains the key upcoming hurdle, amid tight arithmetic favoring a centre-right bloc.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent energy price surges, with year-over-year increases exceeding 12 percent through March 2026, alongside a headline Consumer Price Index (CPI) reading of 3.3 percent for the 12 months ending March—up from prior troughs—have reignited concerns over inflation reacceleration into 2026. The Federal Reserve's preferred Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) gauge hit 3.5 percent in March, prompting upward revisions to end-2026 projections to 2.7 percent PCE in the March FOMC Summary of Economic Projections, amid geopolitical tensions elevating supply risks. Core inflation remains sticky above 3 percent, supported by resilient labor markets and steady consumer spending. Traders eye tomorrow's April CPI release on May 12 for confirmation of the 3.7 percent consensus forecast, ahead of the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, where policy path adjustments could hinge on fresh data.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's administration announced on May 1, 2026, plans to withdraw about 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next six to 12 months, citing tensions with European allies over base access and defense burdensharing, prompting NATO to seek clarification on impacts to alliance deterrence. This move echoes prior threats but stops short of formal NATO denunciation, which Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act prohibits without congressional approval—requiring Senate ratification akin to treaty withdrawal processes. Trader consensus reflects low implied probability due to these legal barriers, historical non-precedent for US exit, and no initiated legislative push, despite ongoing rhetoric. Key watchpoints include NATO summits, congressional hearings on alliances, and further troop realignments.

markets.question: Spread: Mohun Bagan Super Giant (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for April 26 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Mohun Bagan Super Giant" if Mohun Bagan Super Giant win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Inter Kashi FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on indiansuperleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Mohun Bagan Super Giant vs. Inter Kashi FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Indian Super League game, scheduled for April 26 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mohun Bagan Super Giant, sitting second in the ISL 2025-26 table with 13 points from six matches including a +11 goal difference, hold strong home form at Vivekananda Yuba Bharati Krirangan ahead of their rescheduled clash against mid-table Inter Kashi FC on May 12. The Mariners recently drew 0-0 at Bengaluru FC and sit just behind leaders Mumbai City FC, bolstered by attacking depth and midfield control driving their League Shield push. Inter Kashi, with five points from 1-2-3, struggle away, coming off a 1-2 home loss to Mumbai City and facing potential absences like Mario Barco's injury. Playoff implications loom large for the top six, with Mohun Bagan's superior head-to-head edge and recent momentum shaping trader consensus on a competitive yet lopsided matchup.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding lead in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election, held April 16 to replace Gov. Mikie Sherrill, stems from robust Democratic turnout exceeding 135,000 votes—exceptional for a mid-spring special—fueled by national stakes over the House majority. Final county tallies show Mejia at 60.2% (81,825 votes) to Republican Joe Hathaway's 39.4% (53,520), yielding a 20.8-point margin that overperformed 2024 presidential benchmarks in the post-redistricting D+8 district. Weaker performance in Jewish communities like Livingston and Millburn reflected progressive stances on Israel, but broad suburban mobilization secured the hold. Traders' 97% consensus on a 20-25% Mejia margin mirrors these results pending state canvass certification; only substantial uncounted provisionals or a recount could narrow it below 20 points.

markets.question: Will Nicolae Ciucă be the next Prime Minister of Romania?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Romania by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed by the President of Romania and receive a vote of confidence from Parliament, resulting in the official formation of a new government. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister who does not receive a parliamentary vote of confidence will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Romania; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next Prime Minister of Romania?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Romania by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed by the President of Romania and receive a vote of confidence from Parliament, resulting in the official formation of a new government. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister who does not receive a parliamentary vote of confidence will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Romania; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Romania's pro-European coalition government collapsed on May 5 after Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan lost a no-confidence vote, with 281 lawmakers—including Social Democrats (PSD) and far-right parties—ousting him amid disputes over austerity measures. PSD leader Sorin Grindeanu, who spearheaded the motion after his party's coalition withdrawal in late April, now leads trader consensus at 24.5% implied probability, reflecting PSD's parliamentary leverage and his calls for a stable coalition continuation. PNL's Cătălin Predoiu, current deputy prime minister, trails at 16.5% after urging sustained governance, keeping the race tight amid ongoing negotiations. President Nicușor Dan's consultations with party leaders will nominate a candidate needing parliament's majority approval; failure twice could trigger snap elections before November 2028, with coalition pacts or AUR endorsements as key separators.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Los Angeles Angels and Cleveland Guardians combine to score 8 or more runs in this game.

If the combined total is less than 8, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. events.title: Los Angeles Angels vs. Cleveland Guardians
events.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Los Angeles Angels and Cleveland Guardians, scheduled for May 11 at 6:10PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Los Angeles Angels" if the Los Angeles Angels win the game.

This market will resolve to "Cleveland Guardians" if the Cleveland Guardians win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. eventMetadata.context_description: Cleveland Guardians host the Los Angeles Angels in a three-game AL series opener at Progressive Field, where trader consensus reflects the Guardians' home-field advantage and 21-21 record against the Angels' struggling 16-25 mark. Key Angels injuries include catchers Logan O'Hoppe (wrist, 10-day IL) and Travis d'Arnaud (foot, 10-day IL), thinning their lineup depth; Guardians shortstop Brayan Rocchio provides momentum with four hits in Sunday's win over the Twins. Lula holds a slim first-round lead (38.1%–40% vs. Flávio's 34%–37.4% across Ideia, Real Time Big Data, and Futura surveys), but Flávio gains in head-to-heads by consolidating right-wing support from his father's base amid Lula's rising disapproval ratings tied to economic pressures. The deadlock persists due to fragmented third-party votes from figures like Ronaldo Caiado (5%–6%) and undecideds (9%–13%), with upcoming debates, regional shifts in swing states, or scandal resolutions potentially tipping the balance toward a runoff victory for either contender.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lucknow be 44°C on May 12?

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

This market will resolve to the contestant who wins Survivor season 50.

If Survivor season 50 concludes without a winner being declared, or if Survivor season 50 has otherwise not concluded by June 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET this market will resolve to "Other". In the case of a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed contestant whose name comes first in alphabetical order. 

This market will remain open until the conclusion of the season.

The resolution source for this market will be the official broadcast of the final episode of Survivor season 50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Aubry Bracco as Survivor 50 winner at 86.5% implied probability, driven by her survival through late-game chaos—including a historic triple elimination in Episode 6 and blindsides of Ozzy Lusth and Rick Devens—while receiving a subtle "winner's edit" in recent sneak peeks emphasizing information control and resume-building moves. Despite fan criticism of her passive positioning and Cirie Fields' visible alliance leadership (6.5% odds), Aubry's under-the-radar navigation has solidified skin-in-the-game bets, echoing historical patterns where quiet strategists like past runner-ups surge endgame. With Episode 11 airing soon and final immunity challenges looming, any Cirie upset hinges on jury management, but markets see low upset risk absent major twists.

events.title: Galatasaray SK vs. Trabzonspor
events.description: This event is for the upcoming TFF Super Kupa game, scheduled for Friday, May 1, 2026 between Galatasaray SK and Trabzonspor.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus evenly splits at 50% across Galatasaray win, draw, and Trabzonspor victory, capturing the intense Süper Lig rivalry where outcomes remain highly unpredictable despite Galatasaray's dominant season. The league leaders, who clinched a fourth straight title last weekend against Antalyaspor, host at RAMS Park but carry baggage from Trabzonspor's 2-1 upset win in their April 4 head-to-head clash, which fueled the visitors' surge toward third place and Europa League contention. Both sides enter with full squads, minimal injury disruptions—Osimhen recovered for Galatasaray, Onuachu back for Trabzonspor—and end-of-season momentum, with Trabzonspor's recent away resilience offsetting Galatasaray's home dominance in prior encounters.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Miami be between 90-91°F on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Miami Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Miami Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/fl/miami/KMIA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts project a high of 88-89°F at Miami International Airport (KMIA) today, aligning with the 98.2% trader consensus amid partly sunny conditions, southeast winds at 5-10 mph, and a mere 20% chance of afternoon showers or thunderstorms that could cap peak heating. Morning observations show temperatures starting near 81°F under mostly cloudy skies transitioning to sun, consistent with upper-80s model runs from NOAA guidance and historical May normals of 86°F. This positioning reflects strong scientific agreement on boundary layer mixing and sea-breeze effects limiting extremes. Prolonged clear skies and stronger subsidence might push into 90-91°F, while early convection could suppress below 88°F; watch hourly METAR reports and evening NWS climate summary for resolution.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: ONIC PH vs Omega Esports (BO3) - MPL Philippines Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between ONIC PH and Omega Esports in the MPL Philippines Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 15 at 7:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "ONIC PH" if ONIC PH win the match against Omega Esports.

This market will resolve to "Omega Esports" if Omega Esports win the match against ONIC PH.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 36.3% implied probability for Argentina's 2026 annual inflation in the 30.0-34.9% range, closely tracking the Central Bank's latest REM survey median of 30.5% end-year, up 1.4 percentage points from prior estimates. This shift stems from March 2026 CPI rising 3.4% month-over-month—above consensus—pushing year-to-date accumulation to 9.4% amid seasonal education and transport pressures, despite President Milei's austerity measures curbing annual rates to 32.6% from February's 33.2%. Lower bins like 25-29.9% (24.5%) reflect optimism for further fiscal tightening, while upside risks above 40% (24.2% combined) account for potential policy slippage; April CPI data, due imminently, looms as the next catalyst.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran diplomatic engagements remain indirect amid severed formal ties since 1979, with recent direct talks hosted by Pakistan in Islamabad during April 2026 marking the most substantive since the Iranian Revolution. The US delegation, led by Vice President JD Vance alongside special envoys Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff, met Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf, mediated by Pakistani officials. Negotiations stalled over Iran's nuclear program, Strait of Hormuz access, and ceasefire terms, prompting President Trump to dismiss Tehran's counterproposal and convene his national security team—including Vance, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, and CIA Director John Ratcliffe—on May 11 amid hints of military pressure. Pakistan's neutrality faces scrutiny after reports it sheltered Iranian aircraft from US strikes, potentially shifting future venues to Oman or others; traders eye announcements on resumption or escalation.

This market will resolve to 'Luciano Darderi' if Luciano Darderi advances against Alexander Zverev.

eventMetadata.context_description: World No. 3 seed Alexander Zverev holds a clear edge entering the Internazionali BNL d'Italia round of 16 against No. 18 seed Luciano Darderi on clay, bolstered by their 1-0 head-to-head from Rome 2024 (7-6(7), 6-2) and Zverev's 13-3 clay record this year despite recent illness overcome in a straight-sets win over Alexander Blockx. Darderi, riding high after upsetting No. 16 Tommy Paul 3-6, 6-3, 6-2 on May 10 and straight-setting Yannick Hanfmann, brings surging clay form—including a Santiago title in March—and home-crowd energy at Foro Italico as an Italian, marking his first Masters 1000 last-16 appearance. Zverev's superior ranking, serve, and Rome pedigree shape trader consensus, though Darderi's baseline grinding poses upset potential on the slower surface.

eventMetadata.context_description: RCD Espanyol hosts Athletic Club at RCDE Stadium on May 13 in a pivotal La Liga clash, with both sides entangled in the lower mid-table scrap where points could prove decisive against relegation. Espanyol's trader sentiment is weighed down by a dismal winless streak spanning all of 2026—last victory a 2-1 upset at San Mamés against Athletic on December 22, 2025—managing just six points from 48 possible amid a grueling run-in facing Real Madrid, Sevilla, Osasuna, and Real Sociedad. Athletic, sitting slightly higher around 10th-12th with 38-41 points after 33 matches, boasts steadier recent form but grapples with lingering concerns over Nico Williams' groin recovery. Head-to-head favors the hosts lately, yet Espanyol's home form and desperation clash against Athletic's away resilience, with no major injuries reported in the past week.

eventMetadata.context_description: Social Democrats councillor Daniel Ennis dominates trader consensus at 71.5% implied probability for the Dublin Central by-election on May 22, driven by bookmaker odds listing him at 4/9 favorite and expert assessments naming him a likely Condorcet winner under the alternative vote system, bolstered by his recent local election success and focus on poverty and cost-of-living issues. Sinn Féin's Janice Boylan trails at 20%, capitalizing on Mary Lou McDonald's constituency base but facing national headwinds amid intensifying campaigns on housing and crime. Fine Gael's Ray McAdam (8.2%) defends the Paschal Donohoe vacancy, while independent Gerry Hutch (8.4%) draws novelty interest despite gangland associations; recent public meetings and jockeying highlight transfer dynamics as pivotal, with polls showing a fragmented left vote favoring Ennis.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

events.title: What will Gold (GC) hit__ by end of June?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold (GC) June futures trade around $4,725 per ounce, buoyed by robust central bank purchases absorbing 93% of supply and safe-haven demand amid geopolitical tensions and U.S. fiscal deficits, offsetting modestly higher 10-year Treasury yields at 4.41%. The U.S. dollar index (DXY) near 98 supports prices via inverse correlation, while federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75% tempers opportunity costs. Recent March CPI rose 0.9% month-over-month, fueling bets on persistent inflation; traders eye April CPI release May 12 for rate cut signals ahead of the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, where policy guidance could sway end-June settlement amid volatility from real yield shifts.

markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $40 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures for June 2026 trade near $97 per barrel as of May 11, reflecting trader consensus on a persistent geopolitical risk premium from the ongoing US-Iran conflict and intermittent Strait of Hormuz disruptions since late February, which have driven year-to-date gains exceeding 85%. The latest EIA report for the week ended May 1 showed a 2.3 million barrel inventory draw to 457.2 million barrels—slightly above the five-year average—bolstered by steady refinery runs at 16 million barrels per day. OPEC+ countered with a symbolic 188,000 barrels-per-day production hike for June announced May 3, aiming to stabilize markets amid elevated tensions. Key catalysts ahead include weekly EIA storage updates, the May 13 OPEC monthly report, and the June 7 OPEC+ ministerial meeting, where quota adjustments could sway the supply outlook.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. April McClain Delaney's sustained double-digit lead in recent Democratic primary polls—49% to David Trone's 38% in an April survey—bolsters trader consensus on a Democratic hold in Maryland's 6th Congressional District, rated Safe Democratic by Sabato's Crystal Ball and Solid Democratic by Cook Political Report. The district's Montgomery County Democratic suburbs offset western Maryland's GOP lean, where Delaney narrowly prevailed in her 2024 open-seat win. Weak Republican primary contenders like Robin Ficker trail in fundraising, limiting general election threats ahead of the June 23 primaries. Escalating primary rhetoric last week has not eroded the party's structural edge in this midterm cycle.

markets.question: Spread: Hull City AFC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game, scheduled for May 11 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Hull City AFC" if Hull City AFC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Millwall FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Millwall FC vs. Hull City AFC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the EFL Championship game, scheduled for May 11 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the EFL Championship play-off semi-final second leg on May 11 at The Den, 3rd-placed Millwall host 6th-placed Hull City with promotion to the Premier League at stake, following the first leg at the MKM Stadium on May 8. Millwall's 3-1 away victory over Hull on March 7—thanks to goals from Jake Cooper, Macaulay Ivanović, and Josh Coburn—highlights their recent head-to-head edge and strong away form late in the season. Injury concerns persist for both: Hull sidelined by Eliot Matazo's ACL tear and Cody Drameh's absence, while Millwall monitors Josh Coburn's fitness and Caleb Taylor's foot issue. Millwall's higher finish grants home advantage in this decisive leg, amid high-stakes tension and packed schedules.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent U.S. Sen. Jim Risch holds a commanding 96.8% implied probability in trader consensus for the Idaho Republican Senate primary on May 19, driven by his entrenched incumbency advantage, superior name recognition, and fundraising edge in the deep-red state. With ballots finalized last week and early voting underway in counties like Ada and Kootenai, recent coverage highlights low-profile challengers Joe Evans, Denny LaVé, and Josh Roy mounting criticism over Risch's age (83 on Election Day) and tenure, but absent public polls or momentum shifts. Historical patterns show Senate incumbents winning over 90% of primaries; upset scenarios remain slim absent a late scandal, health event, or high-profile endorsement reversal before polls close.

eventMetadata.context_description: Celtic's commanding 79% implied probability stems from their Scottish Premiership pedigree and recent 3-1 Old Firm victory over Rangers on May 10, extending a strong run including wins over Hibernian and Falkirk amid a tight title race where they sit second behind Hearts. Dunfermline's 20.5% upset chance and 21% draw reflect potential from returning striker Zak Rudden and defender Freddie Turley, while Celtic manages absences like Kasper Schmeichel's shoulder issue and Tomas Cvancara's recent groin blow with squad depth.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a potential federal funds rate rise toward 3.75%-4.00% before 2027, mirroring CME FedWatch Tool signals with 45% hike odds by spring 2027 amid resurgent inflation risks. The FOMC maintained its 3.50%-3.75% target range unchanged at the April 28-29 meeting, noting solid economic activity and balanced risks despite low job gains. April nonfarm payrolls rose a softer-than-expected 115,000, while forecasts eye April CPI accelerating to 3.7% annually from March's 3.3%. With 10-year Treasury yields at 4.39%, geopolitical tensions bolster tightening expectations. Watch tomorrow's April CPI data and June 16-17 FOMC projections for shifts.

markets.question: Will France reach the 2026 FIFA World Cup final?
markets.description:

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed nation to advance to the 2026 FIFA World Cup final (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 FIFA World Cup final matchup has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
